claim_chain: We used stranded SNS-seq to identify active origins in T. brucei
The method maps origins between divergent SNS peaks with greater specificity
Origins are not random sequences but have specific genetic elements
DNA replication starts at sites where adenine and thymine are overrepresented in specific patterns
G4 structures near origins are embedded in poly(dA)-rich sequences in strand-specific manner
Origin centres are low nucleosome occupancy regions flanked by high nucleosome occupancy regions
RNA:DNA hybrids show enrichment near origins reflecting RNA-primed SNS molecules

typical_pattern: Method comparison and key benefits statement
Broader implications or connections to field

likely_priorities: Methodological rigor and comparison to existing approaches
Completeness of origin mapping and potential false negatives
Validation across different datasets and strains
Mechanistic interpretation supported by multiple lines of evidence
Generalizability of findings beyond the specific organism
likely_attacks: Stringent selection may miss weak or inefficient origins
Comparing non-exhaustively mapped origins across life stages could be misleading
Elements not found in all origins may reflect strain differences rather than true biology
G4 detection may be influenced by stabilization conditions
Small overlap percentage between RNA:DNA hybrids and origins needs justification
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states 'Some weak or inefficient origins may be missed due to low activity, resulting in low read coverage' and 'It is therefore very likely that the number of origins of this parasite is higher than the number reported here.' It also notes 'we decided not to compare the PCF and BSF sets of origins in more detail because comparing non-exhaustively mapped origins in these two life cycle stages could be misleading.' The authors acknowledge 'The elements presented in our model were not found in all origins' and attribute this to 'the datasets used for the intersections were obtained from four different strains, rather than from just one.' They also discuss G4 detection under 'PDS-stabilised conditions compared to physiological ones' and justify the small overlap by stating 'a small proportion of previously reported RNA:DNA hybrids (1.7%) overlap with SNS-seq origins.'

examples.quoted_phrase: we suggest that these RNA:DNA hybrids represent the SNS-derived RNA:DNA hybrids at the DNA replication initiation sites
Our analysis revealed no consensus sequence associated with the origins
We therefore propose that the formation of G4s plays a regulatory role in the activation of replication origins
It is therefore very likely that the number of origins of this parasite is higher than the number reported here
Our analysis disclosed for the first time a strong link between RNA:DNA hybrid formation and DNA replication initiation

phrase_evidence.phrase: We used stranded SNS-seq to identify a set of active origins
The key benefit of stranded over conventional (non-stranded) SNS-seq is the ability to identify origins with greater specificity
Some weak or inefficient origins may be missed due to low activity
It is therefore very likely that the number of origins of this parasite is higher than the number reported here
Our analysis revealed no consensus sequence associated with the origins
the genomic sites that function as origins are not random sequences
Thanks to the stranded SNS-seq approach, we were able to determine the positions and strand-specificity of some genetic elements
DNA replication starts at genomic sites where adenine and thymine are overrepresented and arranged in a specific pattern
the observed enrichment of G4s around origins cannot be an artefact of the enzymatic treatments
We therefore propose that the formation of G4s plays a regulatory role in the activation of replication origins
the formation of G4 structures could potentially pose or arrest replication fork progression
Our analysis disclosed for the first time a strong link between RNA:DNA hybrid formation and DNA replication initiation
we suggest that these RNA:DNA hybrids represent the SNS-derived RNA:DNA hybrids at the DNA replication initiation sites
we suggest that the summits of RNA:DNA hybrids near the origins reflect the enrichment of RNA-primed SNS molecules
the overlap between the two distinct techniques, DRIP-seq and stranded SNS-seq, provides compelling validation of our origin mapping approach
The elements presented in our model were not found in all origins
This may be caused by the fact that the datasets used for the intersections were obtained from four different strains
comparing non-exhaustively mapped origins in these two life cycle stages could be misleading
consistent with the findings of most other studies of eukaryotic origins
These findings shed new light on the sequence and structural features that define the topology of replication origins
Schematic representation of the experimental workflow
A representative screenshot from Integrative Genomics Viewer (IGV) presenting the read coverage
Violin-plots present the distribution of the lengths of mapped origins
The white line represents the median value
Our methodology is comparable to previously published origin-derived single-stranded DNA sequencing

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based solely on the provided discussion, abstract, and figure legend sections.
